---
title: "Mount swap partition on boot"
date: 2007-08-20
forum: Hardware &amp; Laptops
---

### Post by gletob on 2007-08-20
I need to know how to mount my swap partition at bootup in gnome partition manager it says my linux-swap partition is /dev/sda3 


/etc/fstab
```

# /etc/fstab: static file system information.
#
#  -- This file has been automaticly generated by ntfs-config -- 
#
# <file system> <mount point>   <type>  <options>       <dump>  <pass>

proc /proc proc defaults 0 0
# Entry for /dev/sda2 :
UUID=00f08684-6bb5-4b52-99fa-0147c8d625f7 / ext3 defaults,errors=remount-ro 0 1
# Entry for /dev/sda1 :
UUID=A8ACCD2FACCCF8B8 /media/host ntfs-3g defaults,locale=en_US.UTF-8 0 0
```
thanks in advance

---

### Post by AdotB on 2007-08-20
Adding the following line to your fstab should work:

/dev/sda3	none	swap	sw	0	0

---
